---
title: "FASTRESS 02 Analysis"
format: 
  # uncomment below to transform into html presentation/document. other options: - powerpoint "pptx:" or - pdf document "pdf:" 
  # revealjs:
  #   slide-number: true
  #   theme: default   # simple, default, dark
  html:
    code-fold: true
    embed-resources: true # makes html self contained, including images etc.
    footer: "Project name"
    number-sections: true
    number-depth: 4
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-expand: 3
    toc-location: left
    toc-title: Contents
editor: visual
---

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(tibble)
library(ggplot2)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(readr)

analysis_session <- readRDS("data_clean/analysis_session.rds")
survey_person <- readRDS("data_clean/survey_person.rds")
condition_check <- readRDS("data_clean/condition_check.rds")
stress_person <- readRDS("data_clean/stress_person.rds")
stress_indices_per_session <- readRDS("data_clean/stress_indices_per_session.rds")



```

Im Cleaning File wurde erstellt:Â `subject = factor(fastress_id)`.

-   Im Analyse File sollte direkt nach readRDS einmal gecheckt werden, ob subject und analysis_session wirklich drin sind.

-   ZusĂ¤tzlich prĂĽfen, ob subject wirklich ein Faktor ist

```{r}
stopifnot("subject" %in% names(analysis_session))
stopifnot(is.factor(analysis_session$subject))
stopifnot("planned_condition" %in% names(analysis_session))

```

# Ăśbersicht (Datenstruktur & Analyse-Samples)

**analysis_session**\
Session-Level Datensatz mit einer Zeile pro Person und Session. Zentrale Basis aller Analysen.

**ids_complete**\
Personen mit zwei gĂĽltigen Sessions. Definiert das designkonforme Within-Subject Sample.

**dat_main**\
Complete-Case Sample fĂĽr die Hauptanalyse ohne BerĂĽcksichtigung der Reihenfolge.

**order_tbl**\
Person-Level Tabelle mit Information zur Reihenfolge der Bedingungen.

**dat_main_order**\
dat_main erweitert um die Variable order_group.

**dat_main_final**\
Finaler Analyse-Datensatz fĂĽr alle Hauptmodelle. EnthĂ¤lt planned_condition und order_group als Kovariate. Grundlage fĂĽr Hauptanalysen, Moderationen und SensitivitĂ¤tsanalysen.

**Moderator-Samples**\
dat_main_final gefiltert auf nicht fehlende Moderatorwerte, zB dat_main_srs oder dat_main_sses.

**SensitivitĂ¤ts-Samples**\
Varianten von dat_main_final zur PrĂĽfung der Robustheit, zB ohne Mismatch-Sessions, mit log-Transformation oder ohne obere 1 Prozent Extremwerte.

**blood_wide**\
Wide-Format Datensatz fĂĽr gepaarte Tests der Blutmarker.Analysis

## Analyse Stichproben definieren und dokumentieren

**Was man macht?** Man berechnet, wie viele Personen fĂĽr welche Analyse zur VerfĂĽgung stehen: within Subject Analysen brauchen zwei Sessions pro Person Moderation braucht zusĂ¤tzlich Moderatorwerte

**Wieso man das macht?** So weiss man vor dem Modellieren exakt, welches N man verwendet, und man kann es spĂ¤ter im Ergebnisteil korrekt berichten.

Anzahl Personen insgesamt Anzahl Personen mit zwei Sessions Anzahl Personen mit gĂĽltigen Moderatorwerten

Methodenteil: "Primary analyses were conducted using within subject mixed effects models including only participants with complete data for both experimental sessions. The primary outcome was total energy intake (total_kcal). Planned metabolic condition (fasted vs satiated) was entered as a fixed effect, with a random intercept for participant. Stress reactivity (SRS) was tested as a continuous moderator."

```{r}
# --------------------------------------------------
# Sample sizes for planned analyses
# --------------------------------------------------


# Hauptsample: complete cases fĂĽr within subject Analysen
ids_complete <- analysis_session |>
  filter(!is.na(planned_condition)) |>
  distinct(fastress_id, session) |>
  count(fastress_id, name = "n_sessions") |>
  filter(n_sessions == 2) |>
  pull(fastress_id)

```

## feste Analyse Samples definieren

**Was:** fixe DatensĂ¤tze erstellen, die ich danach konsequent in allen Modellen verwende.

W**arum:** So bleibt das N stabil und ich berichte spĂ¤ter exakt dieselben Samples, die ich wirklich analysiert habe.

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Analyse Setup: fixe Analyse Samples
# ============================================================

# Hauptsample: within subject, complete cases
dat_main <- analysis_session |>
  filter(
    fastress_id %in% ids_complete,
    !is.na(planned_condition),
    !is.na(total_kcal)
  )

dat_main |> count(fastress_id, name = "n_rows_per_id") |> count(n_rows_per_id)
# -> Analysesample ĂĽberprĂĽfen: n = Anzahl Personen, Zeilen pro Person

```

## Stichprobenbeschreibung: Analyse Samples

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Stichprobenbeschreibung 2: Analyse Samples (Sessions und Personen)
# ============================================================

# Gesamt: alle vorhandenen Sessions mit geplanter Condition
analysis_session |>
  summarise(
    n_total_sessions = sum(!is.na(planned_condition)),
    n_total_persons  = n_distinct(fastress_id[!is.na(planned_condition)])
  )

# Designkonformes Hauptsample: complete cases (2 Sessions pro Person) + Outcome vorhanden
dat_main |>
  summarise(
    n_sessions = n(),
    n_persons  = n_distinct(fastress_id)
  )

# Zusatz: wie viele complete cases wurden identifiziert (Personenebene)
tibble(
  n_complete_persons = length(ids_complete)
)

# Tabelle fĂĽr Ergebnisteil
dat_main |>
  group_by(planned_condition) |>
  summarise(
    n_sessions = n(),
    n_persons = n_distinct(fastress_id),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

```

## Carryover / Order Check

PrĂĽfen, ob die Reihenfolge der Bedingungen relevant ist:

-   ob Personen zuerst fasted oder zuerst satiated hatten

-   ob Order einen Haupteffekt hat

-   ob Order die Wirkung vonÂ `planned_condition`Â moderiert

1.  prĂĽfen, ob gleiches Sample wie Haupanalyse. Erwartung: Jede Person hat 2 Zeilen Einmal T1 und einmal T2 Und planned_condition ist nicht NA

```{r}
dat_main |>
  distinct(fastress_id, session, planned_condition) |>
  count(fastress_id) |>
  count(n)

dat_main |>
  count(session, planned_condition)

```

2.  Baue eine Order Variable pro Person Definition: fasted_first bedeutet T1 ist fasted satiated_first bedeutet T1 ist satiated

```{r}
order_tbl <- dat_main |>
  select(fastress_id, session, planned_condition) |>
  mutate(planned_condition = as.character(planned_condition)) |>
  distinct() |>
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = session,
    values_from = planned_condition
  ) |>
  mutate(
    order_group = case_when(
      T1 == "fasted" ~ "fasted_first",
      T1 == "satiated" ~ "satiated_first",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    order_group = factor(order_group, levels = c("satiated_first", "fasted_first"))
  )


order_tbl |> count(order_group)

```

3.  Merge die Order Variable in dat_main Erwartung n_missing_order ist 0

```{r}
dat_main_order <- dat_main |>
  left_join(order_tbl |> select(fastress_id, order_group), by = "fastress_id")

# ============================================================
# QC: Jede Person muss beide Conditions haben
# ============================================================

dat_main_order |>
  group_by(fastress_id) |>
  summarise(
    n_sessions = n_distinct(session),
    n_conditions = n_distinct(planned_condition),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  count(n_sessions, n_conditions)

# Erwartung: 
# n_sessions = 2 -> Jede Person hat zwei Sessions
# n_condiriona = 2 -> Jede Person hat einmal fasted und einmal satiated

dat_main_order |>
  summarise(
    n_rows = n(),
    n_ids = n_distinct(fastress_id),
    n_missing_order = sum(is.na(order_group))
  )

dat_main_order |> count(order_group, session, planned_condition)

# ============================================================
# Finaler Hauptdatensatz fĂĽr Hauptanalysen
# ============================================================
# Auch wenn der Ordereffekt nicht signifikant war, ist es bei einem Cross-over Design sauberer, ihn als Kovariate kontrolliert mitzunehmen.

dat_main_final <- dat_main_order

# = Ab jetzt laufen die Hauptmodelle mit dat_main_final, nicht mehr mit dat_main.

```

4.  Schneller deskriptiver Order Check â€”\> Das ist kein Test, nur ein Blick ob es grob anders aussieht.

```{r}
dat_main_order |>
  group_by(order_group, planned_condition) |>
  summarise(
    n_obs = n(),
    n_ids = n_distinct(fastress_id),
    total_kcal_M = mean(total_kcal, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_kcal_SD = sd(total_kcal, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

```

5.  Modelltest fĂĽr Order Effekt.

    Wir testen drei Modelle\
    A Basismodell ohne Order\
    B plus Order Haupteffekt\
    C plus Interaktion planned_condition mal order_group\
    â€“\> Wenn C gegen B nicht besser wird, gibt es keinen Hinweis, dass der Condition Effekt von der Reihenfolge abhaengt.

```{r}
m_base <- lmer(
  total_kcal ~ planned_condition + (1 | subject),
  data = dat_main_order,
  REML = FALSE
)

m_order_add <- lmer(
  total_kcal ~ planned_condition + order_group + (1 | subject),
  data = dat_main_order,
  REML = FALSE
)

m_order_int <- lmer(
  total_kcal ~ planned_condition * order_group + (1 | subject),
  data = dat_main_order,
  REML = FALSE
)

anova(m_base, m_order_add)
anova(m_order_add, m_order_int)

summary(m_order_int)
confint(m_order_int, method = "Wald")

```

**Interpretation\
**Wenn anova(m_order_add, m_order_int) nicht signifikant ist â€“\> dann kein Hinweis auf Reihenfolge als Moderator des Condition Effekts

Wenn anova(m_base, m_order_add) signifikant ist â€“\> dann gibt es einen generellen Unterschied zwischen fasted_first und satiated_first, unabhĂ¤ngig von Condition

Satz fĂĽr den Methodenteil oder Ergebnisteil Wenn nichts signifikant "Order effects were examined by adding an order factor indicating whether participants completed the fasted condition in the first session. No evidence for order dependent effects was observed."

Wenn signifikant "Order effects were observed, indicating that outcomes differed depending on whether the fasted condition was completed first. Therefore, order was retained as a covariate in all primary models."

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Helper function: Voraussetzungen gepaarter t Tests
# ============================================================

check_paired_t <- function(x, y, label = "difference") {
  d <- x - y
  d <- d[!is.na(d)]

  cat("\n-----------------------------\n")
  cat("Check:", label, "\n")
  cat("-----------------------------\n")

  print(summary(d))
  cat("\nShapiro Wilk Test:\n")
  print(shapiro.test(d))

  boxplot(d, main = paste("Differenzwerte:", label))
  qqnorm(d, main = paste("QQ Plot:", label))
  qqline(d)
}
```

## Demografie Analyse-Sample

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Demografie: Analyse-Sample (dat_main_final)
# ============================================================

# Eine Zeile pro Person im finalen Analyse-Sample
demo_analysis <- dat_main_final |>
  arrange(fastress_id, session) |>
  group_by(fastress_id) |>
  summarise(
    age = first(age),
    sex = first(sex_male_m_female_f),
    education = first(giq10education),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Gesamt N
N_total <- nrow(demo_analysis)

# Alter
age_M  <- mean(demo_analysis$age, na.rm = TRUE)
age_SD <- sd(demo_analysis$age, na.rm = TRUE)
age_min <- min(demo_analysis$age, na.rm = TRUE)
age_max <- max(demo_analysis$age, na.rm = TRUE)

# Geschlecht
gender_tab <- demo_analysis |>
  count(sex) |>
  mutate(percent = round(n / N_total * 100, 1))

# Bildung
edu_tab <- demo_analysis |>
  count(education) |>
  mutate(percent = round(n / N_total * 100, 1))

N_total
age_M
age_SD
age_min
age_max
gender_tab
edu_tab
```

## Manipulationcheck Stress

#### Voraussetzungen paired t-test prĂĽfen

Interpretation

-   Wenn QQ-Plot okay aussieht und keine extremen Ausreisser auffallen: t-Test beibehalten

-   Wenn die Differenzen sehr schief oder stark ausreisseranfĂ¤llig sind:Â Wilcoxon Signed Rank Test -\> zusĂ¤tzlich als SensitivitĂ¤tscheck berichten

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Manipulationcheck Stress: baseline, mild, strong (Session-Level)
# ============================================================

# Nur vollstĂ¤ndige Sessions mit allen drei Stresswerten
stress_check_session <- stress_indices_per_session |>
  filter(
    !is.na(baseline_mean),
    !is.na(mild_mean),
    !is.na(strong_mean)
  )

# QC
stress_check_session |>
  summarise(
    n_sessions = n(),
    n_ids = n_distinct(fastress_id)
  )

# Deskriptive Werte
stress_check_session |>
  summarise(
    n_sessions = n(),
    n_ids = n_distinct(fastress_id),
    baseline_M = mean(baseline_mean, na.rm = TRUE),
    baseline_SD = sd(baseline_mean, na.rm = TRUE),
    mild_M = mean(mild_mean, na.rm = TRUE),
    mild_SD = sd(mild_mean, na.rm = TRUE),
    strong_M = mean(strong_mean, na.rm = TRUE),
    strong_SD = sd(strong_mean, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

# Voraussetzungen prĂĽfen: Differenzwerte
check_paired_t(stress_check_session$mild_mean, stress_check_session$baseline_mean, "Stress mild - baseline")
check_paired_t(stress_check_session$strong_mean, stress_check_session$baseline_mean, "Stress strong - baseline")
check_paired_t(stress_check_session$strong_mean, stress_check_session$mild_mean, "Stress strong - mild")

# Gepaarte t-Tests
t.test(
  stress_check_session$mild_mean,
  stress_check_session$baseline_mean,
  paired = TRUE
)

t.test(
  stress_check_session$strong_mean,
  stress_check_session$baseline_mean,
  paired = TRUE
)

t.test(
  stress_check_session$strong_mean,
  stress_check_session$mild_mean,
  paired = TRUE
)

```

### Manipulationcheck Blutmarker

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Manipulationcheck Blutmarker: fasted vs satiated (paired)
# ============================================================

blood_wide <- analysis_session |>
  filter(fastress_id %in% ids_complete, !is.na(planned_condition)) |>
  select(fastress_id, planned_condition, ketones_pre, glucose_pre) |>
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = planned_condition,
    values_from = c(ketones_pre, glucose_pre)
  )

# QC
blood_wide |>
  summarise(
    n_persons = n(),
    n_ketones_paired = sum(!is.na(ketones_pre_fasted) & !is.na(ketones_pre_satiated)),
    n_glucose_paired = sum(!is.na(glucose_pre_fasted) & !is.na(glucose_pre_satiated))
  )

# Werte robust zu numerisch konvertieren
blood_wide <- blood_wide |>
  mutate(
    across(starts_with("ketones_pre"), ~ readr::parse_number(as.character(.x))),
    across(starts_with("glucose_pre"), ~ readr::parse_number(as.character(.x)))
  )

# Voraussetzungen prĂĽfen
check_paired_t(blood_wide$ketones_pre_fasted, blood_wide$ketones_pre_satiated, "Ketones fasted - satiated")
check_paired_t(blood_wide$glucose_pre_fasted, blood_wide$glucose_pre_satiated, "Glucose fasted - satiated")

# Paired t tests
t.test(blood_wide$ketones_pre_fasted, blood_wide$ketones_pre_satiated, paired = TRUE)
t.test(blood_wide$glucose_pre_fasted, blood_wide$glucose_pre_satiated, paired = TRUE)

```

### Manipulationcheck Hunger

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Manipulationcheck Hunger: fasted vs satiated (paired)
# ============================================================

hunger_wide <- dat_main_final |>
  select(fastress_id, planned_condition, hunger_hunger) |>
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = planned_condition,
    values_from = hunger_hunger
  )
hunger_wide <- hunger_wide |>
  mutate(
    fasted = readr::parse_number(as.character(fasted)),
    satiated = readr::parse_number(as.character(satiated))
  )

# QC
hunger_wide |>
  summarise(
    n_persons = n(),
    n_hunger_paired = sum(!is.na(fasted) & !is.na(satiated))
  )

# Deskriptive Werte
hunger_wide |>
  summarise(
    fasted_M = mean(fasted, na.rm = TRUE),
    fasted_SD = sd(fasted, na.rm = TRUE),
    satiated_M = mean(satiated, na.rm = TRUE),
    satiated_SD = sd(satiated, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

# Voraussetzungen prĂĽfen
check_paired_t(hunger_wide$fasted, hunger_wide$satiated, "Hunger fasted - satiated")

# Paired t test
t.test(hunger_wide$fasted, hunger_wide$satiated, paired = TRUE)


```

## Moderator-spezifische Samples

**Was?** FĂĽr jeden Moderator einen eigenen Analyse-Datensatz erstellen

Interpretation: z.B. 32 Zeilen und 16 IDs -\> dann fehlen keine Moderatorwerte in den complete cases

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Moderator spezifische Samples
# ============================================================

dat_main_srs <- dat_main_final |> filter(!is.na(srs_c))
dat_main_sses <- dat_main_final |> filter(!is.na(sses_c))
dat_main_cerq_adaptive <- dat_main_final |> filter(!is.na(cerq_adaptive_c))
dat_main_cerq_maladaptive <- dat_main_final |> filter(!is.na(cerq_maladaptive_c))
dat_main_stadit <- dat_main_final |> filter(!is.na(stadit_c))
dat_main_edeq <- dat_main_final |> filter(!is.na(edeq_restraint_c))

# RQ2 Sample (kommt erst nach Stress Merge, aber Platzhalter ok)
# dat_rq2 wird spĂ¤ter erstellt

# ============================================================
# Dokumentation der Ns
# ============================================================

sample_n <- tibble(
  sample = c(
    "dat_main_final",
    "dat_main_srs",
    "dat_main_sses",
    "dat_main_cerq_adaptive",
    "dat_main_cerq_maladaptive",
    "dat_main_stadit",
    "dat_main_edeq"
  ),
  n_rows = c(
    nrow(dat_main_final),
    nrow(dat_main_srs),
    nrow(dat_main_sses),
    nrow(dat_main_cerq_adaptive),
    nrow(dat_main_cerq_maladaptive),
    nrow(dat_main_stadit),
    nrow(dat_main_edeq)
  ),
  n_ids = c(
    n_distinct(dat_main_final$fastress_id),
    n_distinct(dat_main_srs$fastress_id),
    n_distinct(dat_main_sses$fastress_id),
    n_distinct(dat_main_cerq_adaptive$fastress_id),
    n_distinct(dat_main_cerq_maladaptive$fastress_id),
    n_distinct(dat_main_stadit$fastress_id),
    n_distinct(dat_main_edeq$fastress_id)
  )
)

sample_n

```

## Outcome Checks und Modellannahmen

**Was:**

-   Verteilungen der Outcomes anschauen,

-   Nullen und Ausreisser prĂĽfen,

-   Residuenchecks fĂĽr das Basismodell.

**Wieso:** Um frĂĽh zu sehen, ob zB. total_kcal stark schief ist oder ob einzelne Sessions extrem sind. Das beeinflusst SensitivitĂ¤tstransformationen und die Interpretation.

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Outcome checks (Verteilungen, Nullen, Ausreisser)
# ============================================================

# Outcomes, die du prĂĽfen willst
outcomes <- c("total_kcal", "high_kcal", "low_kcal", "prop_high_kcal")

# 8.1 Anteil Nullen pro Outcome (nur Sessions mit Condition)
dat_main_final |>
  summarise(across(all_of(outcomes), ~mean(.x == 0, na.rm = TRUE), .names = "p_zero_{.col}"))

# 8.2 Deskriptive Verteilung (Min, Median, Max, Quantile)
dat_main_final |>
  summarise(across(all_of(outcomes),
                   list(
                     n = ~sum(!is.na(.x)),
                     min = ~min(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
                     p25 = ~quantile(.x, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
                     p50 = ~quantile(.x, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),
                     p75 = ~quantile(.x, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
                     p95 = ~quantile(.x, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),
                     max = ~max(.x, na.rm = TRUE)
                   ),
                   .names = "{.col}_{.fn}"))

# 8.3 Top Ausreisser Sessions anschauen (nach total_kcal)
dat_main_final |>
  arrange(desc(total_kcal)) |>
  select(fastress_id, session, planned_condition, total_kcal, high_kcal, low_kcal, prop_high_kcal) |>
  slice_head(n = 10)

# 8.4 Quick Plots (optional, aber hilfreich)
dat_main_final |>
  ggplot(aes(x = total_kcal)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20) +
  facet_wrap(~planned_condition) +
  labs(title = "total_kcal Verteilung nach Condition")

dat_main_final |>
  ggplot(aes(x = planned_condition, y = total_kcal)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "total_kcal Boxplot nach Condition")

```

**Interpretation**: Es gibt VariabilitĂ¤t, aber nicht sofort etwas absurd Unplausibles.\
Die Range ist breit, aber bei Snackintake in einem Laborparadigma noch nicht automatisch problematisch.

## Deskriptive Statistik nach Condition

**Was man macht?** Man schaut sich die Outcomes (total_kcal, high_kcal, low_kcal, prop_high_kcal) nach planned condition an.

**Wieso man das macht?** Das ist der erste PlausibilitĂ¤tscheck fĂĽr den Effekt Man sieht sofort, ob Wertebereiche plausibel sind. Man bekommt Tabellen fĂĽr den Ergebnisteil

```{r}
# --------------------------------------------------
# Descriptives by planned condition
# --------------------------------------------------

#Alle Sessions: explorativer Ăśberblick (nicht fĂĽr Hypothesentests)
analysis_session |>
  filter(!is.na(planned_condition)) |>
  group_by(planned_condition) |>
  summarise(
    n_obs = n(),
    n_ids = n_distinct(fastress_id),
    total_kcal_M = mean(total_kcal, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_kcal_SD = sd(total_kcal, na.rm = TRUE),
    high_kcal_M = mean(high_kcal, na.rm = TRUE),
    high_kcal_SD = sd(high_kcal, na.rm = TRUE),
    low_kcal_M = mean(low_kcal, na.rm = TRUE),
    low_kcal_SD = sd(low_kcal, na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_high_M = mean(prop_high_kcal, na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_high_SD = sd(prop_high_kcal, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )


# Haupttabelle: nur fĂĽr complete cases:
analysis_session |>
  filter(fastress_id %in% ids_complete, !is.na(planned_condition)) |>
  group_by(planned_condition) |>
  summarise(
    n_obs = n(),
    n_ids = n_distinct(fastress_id),
    total_kcal_M = mean(total_kcal, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_kcal_SD = sd(total_kcal, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

```

**Alle Sessions:** explorativer Ăśberblick (nicht fĂĽr Hypothesentests) Beschreibt alle vorhandenen Sessions -\> Manche Personen tragen nur eine Session bei

-\> Das ist ok fĂĽr einen Ăśberblick, aber: fasted und satiated Gruppen bestehen nicht aus denselben Personen Mittelwerte sind nicht direkt vergleichbar =\> dient als explorativer Ăśberblick

**nur complete cases:** Jede Person kommt genau einmal in fasted und einmal in satiated vor. Die Mittelwerte sind within subject vergleichbar =\> designkonforme Beschreibung

Satz fĂĽr Methodenteil: "Primary analyses were conducted on participants with complete data for both experimental sessions, ensuring a within subject comparison of fasted and satiated conditions."

## Random Effects prĂĽfen

**Was:** Testen, ob neben random intercept auch random slope fĂĽr planned_condition sinnvoll ist.

**Warum:** In einem within subject Cross over Design reagieren Personen oft unterschiedlich stark auf fasted versus satiated. Das bildet random slope ab.

Ein Random-Slope fĂĽr planned_condition wird nur ĂĽbernommen,\
wenn das Modell klar besser passt und stabil geschĂ¤tzt werden kann.

-   **erst random intercept als Hauptmodell**

-   dann random slope prĂĽfen

-   random slope nur ĂĽbernehmen, wenn

    -   Modell stabil ist

    -   keine starken Warnungen auftreten

    -   und der Fit sichtbar besser ist

```{r}

# wenn N genĂĽgend gross, prĂĽfen -> sonst weglassen

# ============================================================
# Modellstruktur prĂĽfen: Random Intercept versus Random Slope
# ============================================================

# Vor der finalen Hauptanalyse aktivieren

# m_ri <- lmer(
#   total_kcal ~ planned_condition + order_group + (1 | subject),
#   data = dat_main_final,
#   REML = FALSE
# )

# m_rs <- lmer(
#   total_kcal ~ planned_condition + order_group + (1 + planned_condition || subject),
#   data = dat_main_final,
#   REML = FALSE
# )

# anova_ri_rs <- anova(m_ri, m_rs)
# print(anova_ri_rs)

# summary(m_rs)
# isSingular(m_rs, tol = 1e-4)

use_random_slope <- FALSE

```

## Modelle report ready machen

**Was:** Hilfsfunktionen bauen, die fĂĽr jedes Outcome automatisch Modell, CI, Vergleich und EffektgrĂ¶sse ausgeben.

**Warum:** Copy Paste Fehler sparen und konsistente Outputs.

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Funktionen: report ready Output fuer Mixed Models
# ============================================================

fit_main_effect <- function(dat, outcome, use_random_slope = FALSE) {

  if (use_random_slope) {
    f_m1 <- as.formula(
      paste0(outcome, " ~ planned_condition + order_group + (1 + planned_condition || subject)")
    )
  } else {
    f_m1 <- as.formula(
      paste0(outcome, " ~ planned_condition + order_group + (1 | subject)")
    )
  }

  f_m0 <- as.formula(
    paste0(outcome, " ~ order_group + (1 | subject)")
  )

  m1 <- lmer(f_m1, data = dat, REML = FALSE)
  m0 <- lmer(f_m0, data = dat, REML = FALSE)

  list(m0 = m0, m1 = m1)
}
model_summary_pack <- function(m0, m1, dat, outcome) {

  comp <- anova(m0, m1)
  ci <- confint(m1, method = "Wald")

  beta <- fixef(m1)
  beta_cond <- beta[grep("^planned_condition", names(beta))][1]

  sd_y <- dat |>
    summarise(sd_y = sd(.data[[outcome]], na.rm = TRUE)) |>
    pull(sd_y)

  eff_sd <- beta_cond / sd_y

  list(
    comparison = comp,
    fixed = summary(m1)$coefficients,
    ci = ci,
    eff_sd = eff_sd
  )
}

```

## Basismodell fĂĽr alle Outcomes

(Condition Effekt) **Was man macht?** Man rechnet das erste Mixed Model: Outcome wird durch planned condition vorhergesagt, mit Zufallsinterzept pro Person.

**Wieso man das macht?** Das ist das Referenzmodell. Es zeigt den generellen Unterschied fasted vs satiated. Es ist die Grundlage fĂĽr spĂ¤tere Moderationen

-   `m0`Â ist jetztÂ nicht mehr das reine Nullmodell, sondern dasÂ Baseline-Modell mit Order

-   `m1`Â testet dann, obÂ `planned_condition`Â ĂĽberÂ `order_group`Â hinaus zusĂ¤tzliche ErklĂ¤rung liefert

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Hauptanalyse: Condition Effekt fĂĽr alle Outcomes
# ============================================================

results_main <- list()

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Outcome: total_kcal => RQ1, H1
# ------------------------------------------------------------
m0_total <- lmer(total_kcal ~ order_group + (1 | subject), data = dat_main_final, REML = FALSE)
m1_total <- lmer(total_kcal ~ planned_condition + order_group + (1 | subject), data = dat_main_final, REML = FALSE)

anova(m0_total, m1_total)

results_main[["total_kcal"]] <- list(m0 = m0_total, m1 = m1_total)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Outcome: high_kcal
# ------------------------------------------------------------
m0_high <- lmer(high_kcal ~ order_group + (1 | subject), data = dat_main_final, REML = FALSE)
m1_high <- lmer(high_kcal ~ planned_condition + order_group + (1 | subject), data = dat_main_final, REML = FALSE)

anova(m0_high, m1_high)
summary(m1_high)
confint(m1_high, method = "Wald")

results_main[["high_kcal"]] <- list(m0 = m0_high, m1 = m1_high)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Outcome: low_kcal
# ------------------------------------------------------------
m0_low <- lmer(low_kcal ~ order_group + (1 | subject), data = dat_main_final, REML = FALSE)
m1_low <- lmer(low_kcal ~ planned_condition + order_group + (1 | subject), data = dat_main_final, REML = FALSE)

anova(m0_low, m1_low)
summary(m1_low)
confint(m1_low, method = "Wald")

results_main[["low_kcal"]] <- list(m0 = m0_low, m1 = m1_low)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) Outcome: prop_high_kcal
# ------------------------------------------------------------
m0_prop <- lmer(prop_high_kcal ~ order_group + (1 | subject), data = dat_main_final, REML = FALSE)
m1_prop <- lmer(prop_high_kcal ~ planned_condition + order_group + (1 | subject), data = dat_main_final, REML = FALSE)



anova(m0_prop, m1_prop)
summary(m1_prop)
confint(m1_prop, method = "Wald")

results_main[["prop_high_kcal"]] <- list(m0 = m0_prop, m1 = m1_prop)

```

## Moderation

**Was:** pro Moderator die Interaktion planned_condition mal Moderator testen.

**Warum**: Das entspricht dem Hypothesentest fĂĽr Moderation, sauber vergleichbar ĂĽber alle Moderatoren.

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Moderationen Schritt fuer Schritt: total_kcal
# ============================================================
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Mini QC Funktion: wie viele vollstĂ¤ndige Faelle hat ein Moderationssample
# ------------------------------------------------------------
qc_mod_sample <- function(dat, outcome, cond = "planned_condition", mod) {
  dat |>
    summarise(
      n_rows = n(),
      n_ids = n_distinct(fastress_id),
      n_nonmiss_outcome = sum(!is.na(.data[[outcome]])),
      n_nonmiss_cond = sum(!is.na(.data[[cond]])),
      n_nonmiss_mod = sum(!is.na(.data[[mod]])),
      n_complete_for_model = sum(!is.na(.data[[outcome]]) & !is.na(.data[[cond]]) & !is.na(.data[[mod]]))
    )
}

# --------------------------------------------------
# Guard: stop if no complete cases for the moderation model
# --------------------------------------------------
stop_if_no_cases <- function(qc_tbl) {
  n_cc <- qc_tbl$n_complete_for_model[1]
  if (is.na(n_cc) || n_cc == 0) {
    stop("Keine vollstaendigen Faelle fuer dieses Moderationsmodell. Sample ist leer oder hat NAs.")
  }
}


```

### Moderation 1: SRS

m_srs_add = Haupteffekt Condition plus Haupteffekt Moderator m_srs_int = plus Interaktion anova testet, ob die Interaktion ZusatzerklĂ¤rung bringt

```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Moderation 1: SRS (srs_c)
# Outcome: total_kcal
# Sample: dat_main_srs
# RQ2: H2
# ------------------------------------------------------------

qc_mod_sample(dat_main_srs, outcome = "total_kcal", mod = "srs_c")

m_srs_add <- lmer(
  total_kcal ~ planned_condition + srs_c + order_group + (1 | subject),
  data = dat_main_srs,
  REML = FALSE
)

m_srs_int <- lmer(
  total_kcal ~ planned_condition * srs_c + order_group + (1 | subject),
  data = dat_main_srs,
  REML = FALSE
)

anova(m_srs_add, m_srs_int)
summary(m_srs_int)
confint(m_srs_int, method = "Wald")

```

### Moderation 2: CERQ

Hinweis: CERQ auf 10 setzen

```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Moderation 2: CERQ (cerq_c)
# Outcome: total_kcal
# Sample: dat_main_cerq_adaptive
#         dat_main_cerq_maladaptive
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# CERQ adaptive
qc_mod_sample(dat_main_cerq_adaptive, outcome = "total_kcal", mod = "cerq_adaptive_c")

m_cerq_adapt_add <- lmer(
  total_kcal ~ planned_condition + cerq_adaptive_c + order_group + (1 | subject),
  data = dat_main_cerq_adaptive,
  REML = FALSE
)

m_cerq_adapt_int <- lmer(
  total_kcal ~ planned_condition * cerq_adaptive_c + order_group + (1 | subject),
  data = dat_main_cerq_adaptive,
  REML = FALSE
)

anova(m_cerq_adapt_add, m_cerq_adapt_int)
summary(m_cerq_adapt_int)
confint(m_cerq_adapt_int, method = "Wald")


# CERQ maladaptive
qc_mod_sample(dat_main_cerq_maladaptive, outcome = "total_kcal", mod = "cerq_maladaptive_c")

m_cerq_mal_add <- lmer(
  total_kcal ~ planned_condition + cerq_maladaptive_c + order_group + (1 | subject),
  data = dat_main_cerq_maladaptive,
  REML = FALSE
)

m_cerq_mal_int <- lmer(
  total_kcal ~ planned_condition * cerq_maladaptive_c + order_group + (1 | subject),
  data = dat_main_cerq_maladaptive,
  REML = FALSE
)

anova(m_cerq_mal_add, m_cerq_mal_int)
summary(m_cerq_mal_int)
confint(m_cerq_mal_int, method = "Wald")


```

### Moderation 3: STADI Trait Anxiety

```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Moderation 3: STADI Trait Anxiety (stadit_c)
# Outcome: total_kcal & high_kcal
# Sample: dat_main_stadit
# ------------------------------------------------------------

qc_mod_sample(dat_main_stadit, outcome = "total_kcal", mod = "stadit_c")

m_stadit_add <- lmer(
  total_kcal ~ planned_condition + stadit_c + order_group + (1 | subject),
  data = dat_main_stadit,
  REML = FALSE
)

m_stadit_int <- lmer(
  total_kcal ~ planned_condition * stadit_c + order_group + (1 | subject),
  data = dat_main_stadit,
  REML = FALSE
)

anova(m_stadit_add, m_stadit_int)
summary(m_stadit_int)
confint(m_stadit_int, method = "Wald")

# high_kcal
m_stadit_high_add <- lmer(
  high_kcal ~ planned_condition + stadit_c + order_group + (1 | subject),
  data = dat_main_stadit,
  REML = FALSE
)

m_stadit_high_int <- lmer(
  high_kcal ~ planned_condition * stadit_c + order_group + (1 | subject),
  data = dat_main_stadit,
  REML = FALSE
)

anova(m_stadit_high_add, m_stadit_high_int)
summary(m_stadit_high_int)
confint(m_stadit_high_int, method = "Wald")


```

### Moderation 4: EDEQ & SSES

```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Moderation 4: EDEQ Restraint (edeq_restraint_c)
# Outcome: total_kcal
# Sample: dat_main_edeq
# H5: 
# ------------------------------------------------------------

qc_mod_sample(dat_main_edeq, outcome = "total_kcal", mod = "edeq_restraint_c")

m_edeq_add <- lmer(
  total_kcal ~ planned_condition + edeq_restraint_c + order_group + (1 | subject),
  data = dat_main_edeq,
  REML = FALSE
)

m_edeq_int <- lmer(
  total_kcal ~ planned_condition * edeq_restraint_c + order_group + (1 | subject),
  data = dat_main_edeq,
  REML = FALSE
)

anova(m_edeq_add, m_edeq_int)
summary(m_edeq_int)
confint(m_edeq_int, method = "Wald")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# H5b: Stress Eating (SSES)
# Outcome: total_kcal
# Sample: dat_main_sses
# ------------------------------------------------------------

qc_mod_sample(dat_main_sses, outcome = "total_kcal", mod = "sses_c")

m_sses_add <- lmer(
  total_kcal ~ planned_condition + sses_c + order_group + (1 | subject),
  data = dat_main_sses,
  REML = FALSE
)

m_sses_int <- lmer(
  total_kcal ~ planned_condition * sses_c + order_group + (1 | subject),
  data = dat_main_sses,
  REML = FALSE
)

anova(m_sses_add, m_sses_int)
summary(m_sses_int)
confint(m_sses_int, method = "Wald")




```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Moderationsmodelle speichern
# ============================================================

results_mod_total <- list(
  srs = list(add = m_srs_add, int = m_srs_int),
  sses = list(add = m_sses_add, int = m_sses_int),

  cerq_adapt = list(add = m_cerq_adapt_add, int = m_cerq_adapt_int),
  cerq_mal   = list(add = m_cerq_mal_add,   int = m_cerq_mal_int),

  stadit = list(add = m_stadit_add, int = m_stadit_int),
  edeq   = list(add = m_edeq_add,   int = m_edeq_int)
)


```

Moderationsergebnisse speichern: alle p Werte der Interaktionen in einer Tabelle. -\> perfekt fĂĽr Ergebnisteil und Appendix.

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Tabelle: Moderations Test (add vs int) fĂĽr total_kcal
# ============================================================

mod_tests_total <- tibble(
  moderator = c("srs", "sses", "cerq_adapt", "cerq_mal", "stadit", "edeq"),
  p_int = c(
    anova(m_srs_add, m_srs_int)$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2],
    anova(m_sses_add, m_sses_int)$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2],
    anova(m_cerq_adapt_add, m_cerq_adapt_int)$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2],
    anova(m_cerq_mal_add, m_cerq_mal_int)$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2],
    anova(m_stadit_add, m_stadit_int)$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2],
    anova(m_edeq_add, m_edeq_int)$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2]
  )
)

mod_tests_total


```

## FDR fĂĽr explorative Moderatoren vorbereiten

**ErklĂ¤rung**: Dann sieht man spĂ¤ter direkt:

-   unkorrigierter p-Wert

-   FDR-korrigierter p-Wert

-   ob etwas nach Korrektur noch signifikant ist

Das ist vor allem fĂĽr die finale Interpretation hilfreich.

```{r}
mod_tests_total <- mod_tests_total |>
  mutate(
    p_fdr = p.adjust(p_int, method = "BH"),
    sig_fdr = p_fdr < 0.05
  )

mod_tests_total
```

## SensitivitĂ¤t: planned versus measured mismatch

**Was:** Robustheit testen, indem man Sessions mit Mismatch ausschliesst.

**Warum:** So kann man zeigen, dass die Hauptergebnisse nicht von Non Compliance getrieben sind. Wenn ich nur die metabolisch stimmigen Sessions betrachte, bleibt der Haupteffekt trotzdem bestehen?

**Vorbereitung:** Man braucht measured_condition im Session Datensatz.\
-\> condition_check ist bereits gebaut, also muss man es reinmergen.

```{r}
# ============================================================
# SensitivitĂ¤t: measured_condition und mismatch Flag in analysis_session
# ============================================================

analysis_session2 <- analysis_session |>
  left_join(
    condition_check |>
      select(fastress_id, session, measured_condition),
    by = c("fastress_id", "session")
  ) |>
  mutate(
    mismatch = !is.na(measured_condition) & !is.na(planned_condition) & (measured_condition != planned_condition)
  )

# Hauptsample neu bauen
dat_main2 <- analysis_session2 |>
  filter(
    fastress_id %in% ids_complete,
    !is.na(planned_condition),
    !is.na(total_kcal)
  ) |>
  left_join(
    order_tbl |> select(fastress_id, order_group),
    by = "fastress_id"
  )

## QC
dat_main2 |>
  summarise(
    n_rows = n(),
    n_ids = n_distinct(fastress_id),
    n_missing_order = sum(is.na(order_group))
  )

# SensitivitĂ¤t Sample: ohne mismatch Sessions
dat_main_nomismatch <- dat_main2 |>
  filter(mismatch == FALSE | is.na(mismatch))

# Vergleich Basismodell total_kcal
mods_a <- fit_main_effect(dat = dat_main2, outcome = "total_kcal", use_random_slope = use_random_slope)
mods_b <- fit_main_effect(dat = dat_main_nomismatch, outcome = "total_kcal", use_random_slope = use_random_slope)

cat("\nMit allen Sessions:\n")
print(model_summary_pack(mods_a$m0, mods_a$m1, dat_main2, "total_kcal")$fixed)

cat("\nOhne mismatch Sessions:\n")
print(model_summary_pack(mods_b$m0, mods_b$m1, dat_main_nomismatch, "total_kcal")$fixed)

```

**SensitivitĂ¤t:** Schiefe Verteilung mit log1p

Die Log Transformation macht typischerweise:

-   grosse Werte weniger dominant

-   die Verteilung symmetrischer

-   Residuen oft etwas sauberer

**Was:** log1p(total_kcal) als Robustheitscheck analysiere. Bleibt mein Haupteffekt bestehen, wenn ich die Schiefe der Outcome-Verteilung statistisch abmildere?

**Warum:** Essensdaten sind oft stark rechtsschief, log1p ist eine Standard SensitivitĂ¤t.

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Sensitivitaet: log1p Transformation
# ============================================================

dat_main_log <- dat_main_final |>
  mutate(total_kcal_log1p = log1p(total_kcal))

mods_log <- fit_main_effect(dat = dat_main_log, outcome = "total_kcal_log1p", use_random_slope = use_random_slope)
pack_log <- model_summary_pack(mods_log$m0, mods_log$m1, dat_main_log, "total_kcal_log1p")

pack_log$comparison
pack_log$fixed
pack_log$ci

```

**SensitivitĂ¤t:** Ausreisser Regel a priori

**Was:** Obere Extremwerte markieren, zB ĂĽber dem 99 Prozent Quantil, und das Modell ohne diese Sessions rechnen.

**Warum:** Damit du spĂ¤ter nicht spontan entscheidest, sondern eine klare Regel hast.

```{r}
# ============================================================
# SensitivitĂ¤t: Ausreisser nach 99 Prozent Quantil
# ============================================================

q99 <- dat_main_final |>
  summarise(q99 = quantile(total_kcal, 0.99, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  pull(q99)

dat_main_trim <- dat_main_final |>
  mutate(is_outlier99 = total_kcal > q99)

dat_main_no_out <- dat_main_trim |>
  filter(is_outlier99 == FALSE)

mods_full <- fit_main_effect(dat = dat_main_trim, outcome = "total_kcal", use_random_slope = use_random_slope)
mods_trim <- fit_main_effect(dat = dat_main_no_out, outcome = "total_kcal", use_random_slope = use_random_slope)

cat("\nMit Ausreissern:\n")
print(model_summary_pack(mods_full$m0, mods_full$m1, dat_main_trim, "total_kcal")$fixed)

cat("\nOhne Ausreisser 99 Prozent Regel:\n")
print(model_summary_pack(mods_trim$m0, mods_trim$m1, dat_main_no_out, "total_kcal")$fixed)

```

## Plots: Ergebnisse visuell report ready

**Was:** Spaghetti Plot und Interaktions Plot.

**Warum:** zwei Plot Typen, die man in Resultaten und Anhang braucht.

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Plots: within subject Spaghetti Plot fĂĽr total_kcal
# ============================================================

dat_main_final |>
  ggplot(aes(x = planned_condition, y = total_kcal, group = subject)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "total_kcal pro Person nach Condition", x = "Condition", y = "total_kcal")


# Interaktions Plot ohne Zusatzpakete, simpel ueber Vorhersagen aus dem Modell:
# ============================================================
# Plots: Interaktion planned_condition mal srs_c als Beispiel
# ============================================================

m_srs_int <- lmer(
  total_kcal ~ planned_condition * srs_c + order_group + (1 | subject),
  data = dat_main_srs,
  REML = FALSE
)

# Grid fĂĽr Vorhersagen
srs_seq <- seq(
  from = quantile(dat_main_srs$srs_c, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
  to   = quantile(dat_main_srs$srs_c, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),
  length.out = 50
)

pred_grid <- expand.grid(
  planned_condition = levels(dat_main_srs$planned_condition),
  srs_c = srs_seq,
  order_group = levels(dat_main_srs$order_group)[1]
)

pred_grid$order_group <- factor(
  pred_grid$order_group,
  levels = levels(dat_main_srs$order_group)
)

pred_grid$pred <- predict(m_srs_int, newdata = pred_grid, re.form = NA)

pred_grid |>
  as_tibble() |>
  ggplot(aes(x = srs_c, y = pred, linetype = planned_condition)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Vorhersage total_kcal: Condition mal SRS",
    x = "srs_c",
    y = "predicted total_kcal"
  )

```

## Basismodell Diagnostics

**Was:** Residuen und Fits prĂĽfen, Einflussreiche Punkte markieren.

**Wieso:** Mixed Models sind relativ robust, aber ich will wissen, ob zB ein einzelner Fall den Effekt treibt.

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Modellannahmen und Diagnostics fĂĽr total_kcal (Hauptmodell)
# ============================================================

m_total <- results_main[["total_kcal"]]$m1

# Residuen vs Fits
plot(m_total, which = 1)

# QQ Plot der Residuen
qqnorm(resid(m_total))
qqline(resid(m_total))

diag_df <- dat_main_final |>
  mutate(
    fitted = fitted(m_total),
    resid = resid(m_total),
    resid_z = as.numeric(scale(resid))
  ) |>
  arrange(desc(abs(resid_z)))

diag_df |>
  select(fastress_id, session, planned_condition, total_kcal, fitted, resid, resid_z) |>
  slice_head(n = 10)

```

## Basismodell Report Ready (CI, Modellvergleich, Effektgroesse)

**Was:** Nullmodell vergleichen, Konfidenzintervall fĂĽr Condition Effekt, grobe Effektgroesse berichten.

**Wieso:** Das brauche ich spĂ¤ter 1 zu 1 im Ergebnisteil.

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Basismodell report ready (total_kcal)
# ============================================================

# Nullmodell (nur Random Intercept)
m0_total <- results_main[["total_kcal"]]$m0

# Modellvergleich (Likelihood Ratio Test)
m1_total <- results_main[["total_kcal"]]$m1
anova(m0_total, m1_total)

# Prozent CI fuer Fixed Effects
confint(m1_total, method = "Wald")

# Fixed Effects Tabelle kompakt
summary(m1_total)

# EffektgrĂ¶sse: Condition Effekt in SD Einheiten (bezogen auf dat_main)
sd_total <- dat_main_final |>
  summarise(sd_total = sd(total_kcal, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  pull(sd_total)

beta_cond <- fixef(m1_total)[grep("^planned_condition", names(fixef(m1_total)))][1]
beta_cond / sd_total

```

# Export

```{r}
# # --------------------------------------------------
# # Final sanity checks before export
# # --------------------------------------------------
# 
# stopifnot(exists("dat_main"))
# stopifnot(exists("results_main"))
# stopifnot(exists("results_mod_total"))
# stopifnot(nrow(dat_main) > 0)
# 
# 
# # ============================================================
# # Export: zentrale Outputs speichern
# # ============================================================
# 
# dir.create("outputs", showWarnings = FALSE)
# 
# # Daten
# saveRDS(analysis_session, "outputs/analysis_session.rds")
# saveRDS(dat_main, "outputs/dat_main.rds")
# saveRDS(dat_main_final, "outputs/dat_main_final.rds")
# write_csv(sample_n, "outputs/sample_n.csv")
# 
# # Modelle
# saveRDS(results_main, "outputs/results_main.rds")
# saveRDS(results_mod_total, "outputs/results_mod_total.rds")
# 
# # Tabellen
# write_csv(mod_tests_total, "outputs/mod_tests_total.csv")
# 
# # Session Info
# sink("outputs/session_info.txt")
# sessionInfo()
# sink()

```

```{r}
blood_condition_full |>
  count(support_class)
```
